avoid: Replacing cuBLAS for the full W@X GEMM, as it is already highly optimized and dominates runtime.
Hardcoding hidden dimensions or tensor sizes.
Using fast math flags that could compromise correctness.
evidence: Candidate is 1.2-1.4% slower than baseline across all shapes (speedup 0.985-0.989).
Profile summary indicates bottleneck is rank16_update_or_memory_bandwidth.
Custom kernel uses scalar loads/stores (vector_width=1) and block size 256, likely underutilizing memory bandwidth.
No correctness issues, so performance loss is purely from kernel inefficiency.
recommended_actions: Use vectorized loads/stores (e.g., float4) to improve memory throughput.
Experiment with different block sizes (e.g., 128, 512) to find optimal occupancy and coalescing.
Consider using shared memory to cache tiles of X or intermediate results for better data reuse.
Profile memory bandwidth utilization to confirm bottleneck.
